headline: Algorithmic Transparency Bill Requires Platform Disclosure
context: Parliament introduced Algorithmic Transparency Bill requiring social media platforms to disclose content recommendation systems.

cards.big: Algorithmic transparency bill requires platforms disclose recommendation systems
cards.sub: First law in ASEAN addressing algorithmic accountability in content delivery

cards.big: Platforms must publish quarterly reports on content amplification patterns
cards.sub: Reports must detail how algorithms prioritize political religious and news content

cards.big: MCMC empowered to audit algorithms for bias and manipulation indicators
cards.sub: Platforms have 12 months to comply with disclosure requirements

cards.big: Industry warned full algorithmic disclosure would expose trade secrets
cards.sub: Compromise allows summary disclosure with detailed access for auditors only

cards.big: Algorithm regulation tests whether platform accountability can be achieved without revealing proprietary systems
Algorithmic transparency disclosure required. Platforms must explain how content is amplified, not just what is removed.
